title: Move Dictionary Field Edit from Popup to Side Panel
story: As a DataTools Pro user editing field details in the Dictionary, I want the edit interface to appear in a consistent side panel rather than a DataTables popup, so that the experience matches other panels in the application and feels more natural.
description:
Moves the Dictionary field edit functionality from a DataTables popup modal to a sidebar panel, creating a consistent side-panel editing experience across the application.

The side panel should:
1. Display the name of the object being edited
2. Include a notes field with the note editing interface
3. Remove the metrics section (to be re-added at a later date)
acceptance_criteria: Editing a field in the Dictionary opens a side panel instead of a DataTables popup
The side panel displays the object name
A notes field with editing capability is present in the side panel
The metrics section is removed from the edit interface
The side panel UX is consistent with other sidebar panels in the application
ux_changes: Dictionary field edit moved from a DataTables popup to a right-side panel. Panel shows object name and notes editing. Metrics section removed from the edit interface.
implementation_notes: Part of a broader effort to create consistent sidebar editing experiences across the application. The metrics section was intentionally removed and will be re-added in a future iteration.
comment_summary: Developer implemented and submitted for review. Straightforward UX migration from popup to sidebar.
